Global (All).title: Ransomware attack hits global tech firm
Global (All).summary: A ransomware attack has hit a major global tech firm, disrupting operations and causing significant financial losses.

Global (All).title: Protests escalate in global capital cities
Global (All).summary: Protests have escalated in several global capital cities, highlighting growing tensions and potential for instability.

Global (All).title: Global pandemic-related lockdowns intensify
Global (All).summary: Lockdowns in several countries have intensified in response to a surge in COVID-19 cases, impacting global markets and economies.

United States.title: Supreme Court Abortion Ruling Protest Violence
United States.summary: The recent US Supreme Court ruling on abortion has sparked nationwide protests and violent clashes between demonstrators and law enforcement agencies. The situation remains volatile, with further unrest and potential violent incidents possible in cities across the country.

United States.title: Uvalde Shooting Memorial Day Attack
United States.summary: Tributes are flowing for the victims in Uvalde, Texas, who died in a recent mass shooting on Memorial Day weekend.  A mass attack can occur in any of the U.S., particularly in schools.

United States.title: US Border Violence Spikes
United States.summary: Violence along the US-Mexico border has increased significantly in the past week, with reports of clashes between migrant groups and Border Patrol agents. The situation remains tense, with potential for further escalation.

South Korea.title: Protests at South Korea's National Assembly Amid Labor Reform Debate
South Korea.summary:
Tens of thousands of South Koreans gathered outside the National Assembly building in Seoul to protest against proposed labor reform bills.

The protest, which started on 9th July, marks a continuation of an ongoing dispute between unions and the government over the reform proposals.

The government has been pushing for revisions to the Labor Standard Act to give companies more flexibility in setting working hours, among other changes.

South Korea.title: North Korea Fires Ballistic Missiles into Sea of Japan
South Korea.summary: North Korea fired two ballistic missiles into the Sea of Japan on the 5th of July 2024, causing concern among regional security experts.

South Korea.title: South Korea's President Announces Plans for New National Security Act
South Korea.summary: South Korean President announced plans to pass a new National Security Act, which aims to address concerns related to national security and stability in the country.

Japan.title: Japan sees surge in COVID-19 cases
Japan.summary: A new wave of COVID-19 cases has been reported in Japan, raising concerns about the potential impact on the country's healthcare system.

Japan.title: Japan's economy shrinks for first time in two years, sparking fears of recession
Japan.summary: Japan's economy contracted for the first time in two years, raising concerns about a possible recession in the country.

Japan.title: Japan's government faces scrutiny over its handling of the COVID-19 pandemic
Japan.summary: Japan's government is facing criticism for its handling of the COVID-19 pandemic, with many questioning its response to the crisis.
